title: 24-Hour Home Cage Activity Monitoring in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3po7k","text":"This protocol describes the assessment of spontaneous 24-hour home cage activity in adult mice using infrared beam-break technology. Beam crossings are used as a continuous, non-invasive indicator of horizontal locomotion across the full light–dark cycle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5h3jd","text":"•       Do not disturb cages during recording to avoid artifactual activity bursts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"47jef","text":"•       Cage changes or husbandry procedures should be completed before recording starts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65uqm","text":"•       Confirm no beam obstructions (e.g., food pellets blocking infrared beams) before starting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0ps1","text":"•       Acclimate mice to the procedure room for ≥30 min before housing in activity cages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ee45i","text":"•       Ensure the infrared beam frame is calibrated and beams are unobstructed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egias","text":"•       Confirm automated light timer is set: lights OFF at 19:00, lights ON at 07:00.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au4uh","text":"•       Label cages with anonymous IDs to allow blinded data collection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bpmcs","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a43o7","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4n2jt","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":""},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":[140,"444"],"data":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eCages\u003c/b\u003e","20 x 30 cm2 transparent cages "],["\u003cb\u003eMonitoring system\u003c/b\u003e","Infrared beam frame (3-beam horizontal setup)"],["\u003cb\u003eData acquisition\u003c/b\u003e","Activity recording software (30-min bin intervals)"],["\u003cb\u003eHousing\u003c/b\u003e","Ad libitum access to food and water"],["\u003cb\u003eLighting\u003c/b\u003e","Automated 12 h light / 12 h dark cycle (lights off 19:00, on 07:00)"]],"guid":"9C4D156136EE4981A850145B3E626F13","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eCages\u003c/b\u003e","20 x 30 cm2 transparent cages "],["\u003cb\u003eMonitoring system\u003c/b\u003e","Infrared beam frame (3-beam horizontal setup)"],["\u003cb\u003eData acquisition\u003c/b\u003e","Activity recording software (30-min bin intervals)"],["\u003cb\u003eHousing\u003c/b\u003e","Ad libitum access to food and water"],["\u003cb\u003eLighting\u003c/b\u003e","Automated 12 h light / 12 h dark cycle (lights off 19:00, on 07:00)"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Place mice individually in 20 x 30 cm 2 transparent cages located between the photo beams.

Start the activity recording software around noon (12:00). Confirm beam-break counts reset every 30 min. Verify that the 30-min bin intervals are logging correctly. Recording should begin during the light phase so that one full light and one full dark phase are captured in the 24 h window.
Allow automated lighting to switch: lights OFF at 19:00 (onset of dark/active phase) and ON again at 07:00 the following morning.
After 24 h of continuous recording, stop data acquisition and export beam-break counts per 30-min bin for each animal.

Export counts per bin per animal. Group into light-phase (07:00–19:00) and dark-phase (19:00–07:00) totals. Apply appropriate statistical tests (e.g., two-way ANOVA with genotype × phase as factors). Mice are nocturnal; the majority of activity is expected during the dark phase. Total beam crossings in the dark phase should substantially exceed those in the light phase in healthy controls.
creator: Sarah van Veen
